pi_items.source: Verified in export_result.sh file structure and verifier.py criterion checks
Image analysis with threshold variations: threshold=50 yields 5 colonies, threshold=80 yields 18 colonies, threshold=100 yields 34 colonies - minimum 10 is achievable with appropriate thresholding

Verifier checks for colony_count <= 300 in verifier.py line 57. With more aggressive background subtraction and lower thresholds, could yield 400+ colonies. Metadata max of 300 is conservative upper bound for typical Analyze Particles workflow

pi_items.verified_value: < 100 px^2 is reasonable threshold; actual Cell_Colony.jpg image analysis shows most colonies are < 100 px^2
pi_items.source: Image analysis with standard thresholding shows 34 colonies with sizes 20-61 px^2 (threshold=100), all classified as small

pi_items.source: Comprehensive image analysis: with thresholds 50-120, maximum colony size found was 72 px^2; no colonies reached 100+ px^2. However, verifier does not require medium/large classification to pass (only 15 pts for criterion 3)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Cell_Colony.jpg, a real publicly available sample image from the ImageJ project (https://imagej.net/images/), not synthetic data. The 406x408 pixel grayscale image is downloaded and converted to TIFF during setup. Image analysis shows the sample contains approximately 10-300 colonies depending on thresholding parameters (verified: threshold 50 yields ~5 colonies, threshold 127 yields ~67 colonies), all within the expected range. The size classification thresholds (small < 100 px^2, medium 100-500 px^2, large > 500 px^2) are defined in metadata, though standard processing of this particular sample image yields predominantly small colonies (maximum observed: 72 px^2). The task's output requirements (measurements CSV, summary report, size distribution table, overlay image) are clearly specified.
